Basic form of the exception particle

steps.m: to think or assume without certainty

Adding vowel marks to make it a complete past tense verb

steps.n: Changed from 'he thought' (verb) to 'the assumption' (noun) by adding ٱل (the) at start. The fatha at end shows it's the object being followed.

steps.m: and what/that which (connects to يَتَّبِعُونَ - they follow)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement. This shows that 'what they follow' is connected to the previous mention of them following assumptions.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show present tense for 'she'. The word الْأَنفُسُ (the souls) is doing this action in the verse.

Changes from root meaning into noun meaning 'self/soul'

Adds ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action in تَهْوَى (desires/inclines to)

Added و to connect this phrase with what came before it
Basic meaning of the root

Added هُم (them) to show who received the action

Adding damma to help connect with next word

steps.m: to guide or show the way
steps.n: Basic meaning of the root
Changes from root to noun form meaning guidance
Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the guidance' instead of just 'guidance'. The ٰٓ is just a pronunciation mark.
This word connects to the previous verse to ask 'or do humans get whatever they wish for?' It shows disbelief or rejection of this idea.
The root conveys the basic idea of social interaction and companionship
Root 'to be social' becomes a noun meaning 'human' (because humans are social beings)
Adds the preposition that indicates purpose or destination
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'

steps.m: for/to the human being (object of أَمْ)
steps.n: The word gets a kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after the preposition لِ (meaning 'for/to')
This is a relative pronoun that refers to what is desired/wished for. In this ayah, it connects to تَمَنَّىٰ (he desires/wishes) to mean 'whatever he desires'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense form
Doubling of middle letter intensifies the basic meaning
Adding تَ creates Form V, making the intense desire reflexive (directed at oneself)
This is the unique name of God in Arabic, derived from the root meaning divinity
Adding ل at the beginning shows something belongs to or is for Allah
Adding the vowel marks helps with pronunciation and shows the grammatical role
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows from something before it

steps.m: to come after, to be later

steps.n: Changes from basic root to show 'the one who/that which comes last' - like saying 'the ending one'
Adds feminine ending to make it refer to a feminine noun
Definite article makes the noun definite/specific
Damma shows this word is the subject in relation to فَلِلَّهِ (then to Allah belongs...)
Changed root letters to make feminine word meaning 'first' by adding special ending ى with marks above letters
Added ال with hamzat wasl (ٱ) to make it definite
Added 'and' (و) at the start to connect with previous word
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: and how many (introduces a rhetorical question about مَّلَكٍ/angels)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This combination creates a rhetorical question about the number of angels.
Basic preposition showing where something starts or what it's made of
Added shadda for emphasis because this word comes after وَكَم (and how many) to stress the large number being discussed
The root letters for 'rule/own' get short vowels (fat-ha) to make a new word meaning 'angel'
Adding shadda for emphasis and pronunciation
Adding tanwin kasra shows this is indefinite (any angel) and is being described by what follows in the heavens
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows the angels are located in the heavens.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added hamza to make it a noun meaning 'sky' or 'heaven'
Added وات (waat) to make it plural, meaning multiple heavens/skies
Added ال to make it definite ('the heavens')
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition في (in/at) in the ayah
Basic negative particle that negates the verb تُغْنِى (will benefit) that comes after it in this ayah. This shows that the intercession will not benefit.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he benefited' to 'she/it benefits' to match with 'their intercession' (شَفٰعَتُهُمْ) which is feminine. The تُ at the start shows it's happening now.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he helped someone' into 'the act of helping others' by adding alif and round taa at the end
Changed taa marboota to regular taa and added damma to show it's the subject
Added هُمْ (hum) at the end to mean 'their' + changed middle alif to alif with line on top

steps.m: anything/something (object of تُغْنِى - does not avail)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action. This word is what the intercession does not avail/benefit.
Basic exception particle that shows something is being excluded from what was mentioned before

steps.m: except (excluding what follows from لَا تُغْنِى - does not benefit)
steps.n: Added shadda and alif to strengthen the exception meaning. In this ayah, it excludes what comes after from the statement that intercession 'does not benefit'
Basic preposition showing where something starts. Here it connects with the word بَعْدِ (after) to show the starting point of time.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and create the basic noun meaning 'after'. This word is used with أَن (that) to mean 'after that'.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (أَن)

steps.m: that (connects to يَأْذَنَ - He gives permission)
steps.n: This particle connects two parts of a sentence, showing that something happens after something else. Here it connects to the verb يَأْذَنَ (He gives permission), showing what happens after the angels' intercession.
This is the basic concept of giving permission or allowing something to happen
Adding the vowels changes it from a concept to a specific action that happened

steps.m: he permits/allows (subject of يَشَآءُ - he wills)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start. The middle vowel changes to sukun. This word connects to يَشَآءُ (he wills) in the ayah, showing who Allah permits.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and emphasizing the ل with a shadda. The ُ shows this is the subject doing the permitting in this verse.
Compound of preposition 'li' (for) and relative pronoun 'man' (who/whom)
Added fatha marks and the middle weak letter ي changed to ا, which is common in hollow verbs (verbs with a weak middle letter)
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, and ُ at the end to show it's happening now. The آ (long alif with hamza) is because this is a hollow verb.

steps.m: to be pleased with or approve of

steps.n: Basic past tense form showing someone felt pleased
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start. The ending changes to ى because it's a special verb that ends in ى in the present tense
Added و at beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Added vowel marks to show it's present tense
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes how the disbelievers name the angels.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال ('the') turns 'who/which' into 'the one who/which', making it point to something specific

steps.m: those who (plural, referring to the disbelievers mentioned in لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Adding ين makes it plural, showing we're talking about multiple people. This refers to the disbelievers mentioned right after in the ayah who don't believe in the hereafter.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) negative, changing it to 'they do not believe'. It's like adding 'not' in English to make something negative.

steps.m: to trust or believe in something/someone
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to show someone actively choosing to believe in religion
Changed to present tense with yu- prefix and appropriate voweling
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action
This is the basic meaning of the root
Changed the verb into a word describing something that comes after other things

steps.m: the life that comes after (this life)

Added shadda to show emphasis and connected letters with fatha
Added يُ at the beginning to show someone is receiving the action of naming (changes from doing the naming to being named)
Added ون to show multiple people (they) are being named instead of just one person
Added لَ at the start for emphasis. In this ayah, they are naming the angels (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةَ).

steps.m: to send messages, to communicate between parties
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb

steps.m: the angels (object of يُسَمُّونَ - they name)

This is the basic meaning of the root

Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُسَمُّونَ (they name)
This is the basic meaning of the root

Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. Here مَا means 'not' because it's being used with لَهُم (they have) to say 'they do not have.'

steps.m: except/only (excluding ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا from what came before)

Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word ٱلْعِلْمِ (knowledge)

This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that 'your Lord' (رَبَّكَ) definitely knows who has gone astray.

steps.n: Added the preposition بِ at the beginning to show that this word connects to أَعْلَمُ (most knowing). It's like saying 'most knowing about/of whoever' in English.

steps.n: Added fathas to make it a past tense verb about one person (he)
When two same letters come together at the end of a word, they combine with a shadda. This word is connected to أَعْلَمُ (most knowing) in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows movement or distance away from something. In this ayah, it connects to سَبِيلِهِ (His path) to show who strayed away from His path.

steps.m: to travel a path or way

Added vowel marks to show this is a complete pronoun meaning 'he'. This word is the subject of أَعْلَمُ (most knowing) in this ayah.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show he did this action for himself
Added ى at the end to complete the past tense form
This is the base word referring to God in Arabic. It's a unique word that doesn't come from a typical root.
Added ل at the beginning to show something belongs or relates to Allah. The ا from ال is removed when ل is added.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being affected by another word in the sentence.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and'.
This is a relative pronoun that connects to what follows it. Here it refers to everything that exists in the heavens.

steps.m: in/within (connects السَّمٰوٰتِ to what comes before)

Changed from a verb meaning 'rise up' to a noun meaning 'sky' by adding a long a sound and hamza at the end

steps.m: to give what is due (reward or punishment)
steps.n: The root's weak letter ي stays as is in this form

Adding ال makes it specifically refer to someone or something
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people

steps.m: those who (subject of يَجْزِىَ - will reward)

Added short vowel marks (fat-ha) to make it mean 'he was bad' in the past tense
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing something to happen. Changed vowel after س to sukun.
